title: NPC soul file (authored birth-state, v1)
description: The authored starting state of an NPC soul. This file is the template the mind daemon (Pan) instantiates from and the memory store is seeded from. Runtime soul state — memories formed in play, emotional drift, relationship deltas — lives in save data (SP) or the server (MMO), never here. v1 adds mind tier, memory seeds, emotional baseline, and the relationship graph; every v0 file is a valid v1 file (all new fields optional).

soul_version.description: Contract revision this file is authored against. Omitted = 0.

mind.description: Which Pan provider runs this soul — the robots-vs-droids tier (GAME-DESIGN.md §6.2) as a data field. Omitted = rules (a background NPC). Moddable like everything else.

memory_seeds.description: Authored starting memories, ingested into the soul's vault at instantiation. Written in first person, past tense — they read back as the NPC's own recall.

emotional_baseline.description: Named emotional axes and their resting values (-1..1). Runtime state drifts around these and decays back toward them. Axis vocabulary lives in the profile doc, not the schema — mods may add axes.

relationships.description: Initial edges of the relationship graph (to other NPCs or `player`). Runtime deltas live in saves.

allegiances.description: Faction or group id -> starting disposition, -100 (sworn enemy) to +100 (would die for).

extra.description: Free-form escape hatch for mod-specific data. Never read by the engine.

station.description: Interior room this crew member is stationed in (must name a room in the ship's interior_rooms). Read by CrewRoster; optional — the first hull room is the fallback.

color.description: Stand-in sprite color (hex) for this character until sprite art lands. Read by ShipInterior and other framework scenes; optional — a deterministic per-id color is the fallback.

faction.description: Faction this NPC belongs to. Used for patrol/combat contexts. Optional — omitted for unaffiliated characters.

aboard.description: True when this crew member is currently serving aboard their ship (CrewRoster membership). Authored-but-ashore characters (future hires, story joins) omit it or set false.

synthetic.description: True for droids and robots. Synthetic crew do not need cryosleep during self-generated jumps (GAME-DESIGN.md §6.3) and stay conscious through hyperspace transit. Omitted = organic.

jump_pilot.description: True for the crew member who pilots the ship through self-generated hyperspace jumps while the organic crew sleeps. Must be synthetic. Read by the CryoTransit sequence; the first aboard synthetic is the fallback.

playable.description: Marks this crew member as a selectable player character. The character-select screen reads everything here; the engine reads stats generically (piloting/engineering/medicine/grit/savvy, 1..5). A dialogue this NPC hosts cannot play when the player IS them — self_dialogue_summaries maps dialogue id → a second-person narration card shown instead (the beat still completes).

tagline.description: One line under the name on the select screen.

stats.description: Named stats 1..5. Framework vocabulary: piloting (flight handling), engineering (repair speed & power trim), medicine (triage — fewer/softer interior casualties of combat), grit (hull soak & steadiness in zero-G), savvy (better shop prices).

scrawl.description: Paragraphs of the opening text scrawl when starting as this character. Appended to the manifest's shared start.scrawl.

self_dialogue_summaries.description: dialogue id → what happens instead when the player IS this character: a second-person narration card plus the mutations the skipped scene would have applied (story flags must still land). The host reports dialogue_end so missions advance.

mutations.description: Dialogue-schema mutations applied when the card shows.

locomotion.description: How this character moves through gravity regimes. zero_g 'magnetic' walks normally in zero-G (mag-locked feet); 'drift' floats on inertia. gravity_speed_mult scales walk speed under gravity (a heavy chassis crawls).

repairs.description: This crew member seeks out and repairs interior ship damage on their own.
